other_observers: None; Rich Miller found this bird on 4 August and he saw it again on 5 August in the same location as I saw it and also he saw it on low flights of short distance. Bryan Patrick saw the birds on 6 August at 0700 flying for three seconds in the same location.
specific_location: Holcim Marsh a few miles east of Florence.
description: I went to look for a previously reported Least Bittern. I saw the bird twice, very briefly both times in flight. I saw a small heron with buffy wingbars from the dorsal view that had a light, but a proportionately long bill. The sides of the head and its outstreched neck were rufous and it had a black cap. The underwings were medium gray and the feet extended past the tail in flight. I didn't really see anything else in the time the bird was in view.Behaviors: Twice in a very low and short flight over a cattail marsh, Holcim Marsh.Call: No vocalization heardPlumage: possibly juvenile but unknown
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard male LEBI
